info.description: The Direct Initiation Payment API allows consumers to execute direct-initiated payments on accounts for which a grant has been given to the API consumer.
info.title: Direct Initiation Payment API

license.name: Copyright (c) ING Bank N.V. All Rights Reserved.

tags.name: direct-initiated-payment
tags.description: Execute a single direct-initiated payment for all active European ING online payment accounts (SEPA Credit Transfer, Domestic Credit Transfer or True International Payment)

tags: Initiate One-time Payment (Credit Transfer) in Direct Initiation mode
post.summary: Initiate Direct Payment Order (Credit Transfer) for External consumers via Gateway
post.description: API to initiate a credit transfer payment with single or multiple transaction with ISO 20022 pain.001.001.10 and status and status reason will be ISO compliant in the response.

parameters.name: X-Request-ID
parameters.description: ID of the request, unique to the call, as determined by the initiating party.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer Token. Represents Application Access Token or Customer Access Token, depending on the use case. The process to obtain the Application Access Token is described in Client Credentials "Authentication Flow": https://developer.ing.com/openbanking/get-started

parameters.name: Digest
parameters.description: The "Digest" Header header as defined in [RFC3230] contains a Hash of the message body. The only hash algorithms that may be used to calculate the Digest within the context of this specification are SHA-256 and SHA-512 as defined in [RFC5843].

parameters.name: X-JWS-Signature
parameters.description: As specified in https://www.openbankingeurope.eu/media/2096/obe-json-web-signature-profile-for-open-banking.pdf, it consists of: 1. A JSON Web Signature Protected Header, 2. Data to be Signed (not present i.e. empty, as we have detached payload), 3. The JWS Signature Value.

parameters.name: Content-Type
parameters.description: Used to specify the content type of the request data.

get.summary: Get payment status and details for Direct Initiated Payment Order (Credit Transfer) for consumers
get.description: API to get payment status & details for direct-initiated payment order based on the unique identifier of the payment (paymentId), as provided by ING as part of the response of the payment initiation request

parameters.name: X-Request-ID
parameters.description: ID of the request, unique to the call, as determined by the initiating party.

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Bearer Token. Represents Application Access Token or Customer Access Token, depending on the use case. The process to obtain the Application Access Token is described in Client Credentials "Authentication Flow": https://developer.ing.com/openbanking/get-started

parameters.name: paymentId
parameters.description: Identification of payment instruction in ING (resource identification of the generated payment initiation resource)

message.example: Unexpected end of file (missing a terminator/close bracket?)

message.example: Unexpected end of file (missing a terminator/close bracket?)
message.description: A descriptive error message for the consumer

source.description: Optional source descriptor for this error

target.description: Key/value pair indicating which input property caused the error

MoneyTransferResponse.description: Response object containing the usecase and payload for the prepared money transfer

ExternalPaymentTransactionStatus1Code.description: ISO status of the Order or Transaction

INGGlobalReferencesV02.description: The UUID is a very unique identifier, used abundantly by operating systems, disk management systems, databases and applications to uniquely tag objects. The UUID algorithms are well known, and these are implemented in most programming languages as internal libraries. Also, multiple databases are able to generate UUID's on-the-fly. The UUID format consists of 36 lower-case, hexadecimally filled positions, with fixed hyphens on position 9, 14, 19, 24 xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx. The division in bytes is 8-4-4-4-12. The values are stored in Big Endian byte order (significant byte first)

loginMeansCountryCode.description: Country of selected Login Means e.g. RO(for Retail Romania), WB (for ALL WholeSale bank countries)

instdPdctTp.description: The addressed payment product endpoint. The list of products supported is  - sepa-credit-transfers - cross-border-credit-transfers - domestic-credit-transfers - instant-credit-transfers

requestedExecutionType.description: Specifies the instructed execution type of Payment

instantExecutionPossible.description: True / False: based on the instant processing capability as determined in payment initiation process

ingInternalTrxClssfctn.description: IITC code - valid value range is provided in an externally maintained list.

startDate.description: The first applicable day of execution starting from this date is the first payment. Expressed in YYYY-MM-DD format

endDate.description: The last applicable day of execution If not given, it is an infinite standing order. Expressed in YYYY-MM-DD format

reqInf.description: Information supplied along with payment request

authorizationMeansName.description: Name of AUTHORIZATION_MEANS used for Authorization of Payment

authorizationMeansID.description: ID of AUTHORIZATION_MEANS used for Authorization of Payment

forcdBook.description: 'True': Forced booking required. 'False' or not available: Forced booking not required.

zeroTariffChrg.description: 'True': Zero Tariff Charging required. 'False' or not available: Zero Tariff Charging not to be applied.

zeroTariffBllg.description: 'True': Zero Tariff Billing required. 'False' or not available: Zero Tariff Billing not to be applied.

accptncDtTm.description: Transaction acceptance date time - To allow an accurate control of the maximum execution time by all parties involved in the SCT Inst Transaction the Originator Bank has to add a Time Stamp in the SCT Inst Transaction marking the start of the Execution Time Cycle

txSts.description: Specifies the status of a transaction, in a coded form.

txStsRsnInf.description: Status reason information about transaction status

docPresntgPty.description: CTRF: Customer Credit Transfer (MT103 credit leg). FTRF: Financial Institution Transfer (MT202 credit leg). RTRF: Request For Transfer (MT101 debit leg).

cardSeqNb.description: Card sequence number. use case deposits

receiptId.description: Receipt ID with respect to bank deposits

cardAccptrTxId.description: Unique transaction ID with respect to bank deposits

retrRefNum.description: Retrieval Reference Number (RRN) - Unique transaction ID with respect to cash transactions

storeNb.description: Store number - Branch number of the customer

maskedPAN.description: Masked PAN as created in the case of Cash Deposits by CashOrchestration

clearingCycleId.description: Clearing Cycle identifiers as created in the case of Cash Deposits by CashOrchestration

externalSource.description: The name of the external source e.g. LIOM or GIOM

aspspScaApproach.description: ASPSPScaApproaches supported by the third party (bank)

redirectBackURL.description: RedirectBackURL from the third party (bank) (in case of REDIRECT SCA)

messageToPSU.description: Message from the third party (bank) (in case of DECOUPLED SCA)

scaMethod.description: SCAMethod from the third party (bank)(in case of EMBEDDED SCA)

frequencyCode.description:
The following codes from the "EventFrequency7Code" of ISO 20022 are supported.
- DAIL: Daily
- WEEK: Weekly
- TOWK: EveryTwoWeeks
- MNTH: Monthly
- TOMN: EveryTwoMonths
- FOMN: EveryFourMonths
- QUTR: Quarterly
- SEMI: SemiAnnual
- YEAR: Annual

dayOfExecution.description:
Day of execution as string in the form DD.
This string consists always of two characters.
31 is ultimo of the month.

executionRule.description:
"following" or "preceeding" supported as values.
This data attribute defines the behavior when recurring payment dates falls on a weekend or bank holiday.
The payment is then executed either the "preceeding" or "following" working day.

properties.description: Array of properties for more details

ExternalMessage.description: An INFO, WARNING or ERROR message with a code, textual message and possibly a payload object.

TransactionIndividualStatus3Code.description: Code    Name  Definition ACCC  AcceptedSettlementCompletedCreditorAccount  Settlement on the creditor's account has been completed. ACCP  AcceptedCustomerProfile  Preceding check of technical validation was successful. Customer profile check was also successful. ACFC  AcceptedFundsChecked  Preceding check of technical validation and customer profile was successful and an automatic funds check was positive. ACSC  AcceptedSettlementCompletedDebitorAccount  Settlement on the debtor's account has been completed. Usage : this can be used by the first agent to report to the debtor that the transaction has been completed. Warning : this status is provided for transaction status reasons, not for financial information. It can only be used after bilateral agreement ACSP  AcceptedSettlementInProcess  All preceding checks such as technical validation and customer profile were successful and therefore the payment initiation has been accepted for execution. ACTC  AcceptedTechnicalValidation  Authentication and syntactical and semantical validation are successful ACWC  AcceptedWithChange  Instruction is accepted but a change will be made, such as date or remittance not sent. ACWP  AcceptedWithoutPosting  Payment instruction included in the credit transfer is accepted without being posted to the creditor customer’s account. CANC  Cancelled  Payment initiation has been successfully cancelled after having received a request for cancellation. Usage: code to be used in the context of APIs only. PATC  PartiallyAcceptedTechnicalCorrect  Payment initiation needs multiple authentications, where some but not yet all have been performed. Syntactical and semantical validations are successful. PDNG  Pending  Payment initiation or individual transaction included in the payment initiation is pending. Further checks and status update will be performed. RCVD  Received  Payment initiation has been received by the receiving agent. RJCT  Rejected  Payment initiation or individual transaction included in the payment initiation has been rejected.
